premise: I worked 30 years as my brother’s bookkeeper in his construction company, routinely deferring my own salary to keep the business afloat on his promise I’d be a 50% partner when he retired. The day I turned 60, he terminated me, claimed I’d always been just an hourly employee, and gloated that he’d already accepted a $2.4 million buyout from a private-equity firm. I discovered he’d never paid my Social Security taxes for over a decade, tipped off the IRS, and triggered enough back-taxes and fraud penalties to kill the sale. Then I bought the company’s distressed debt from the bank for $40,000, restructured the firm under my sole ownership, and now he’s my subcontractor — required to hand me weekly progress reports in the office where he once fired me.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Brother Fired Me At 60 After I Deferred Salary For 30 Years. So I Became His
